q1_justification: The Ares Program is a cooperative civilian scientific mission with no mention of conflict or competition between nations or factions; the only struggle is against the hostile Martian environment.
q2_justification: The crew occupies a Hab designed to last 31 days and uses an MAV to return to orbit; the mission is explicitly temporary with no permanent settlement intended.
q3_justification: Hermes traveled 124 days to reach Mars, representing a multi-month separation from Earth that is meaningful but under one year one-way.
q4_justification: The crew speaks casual American English, references NASA and Wikipedia, and Mars operations are essentially Earth society and culture transported to another planet with survival gear.
q5_justification: Watney's log is written in casual American English with no indication of any linguistic differences between Earth-based and space-based communication.
q6_justification: Mars requires constant technological intervention to survive: a pressurized Hab, space suits with life support, an oxygenator, water reclaimer, and controlled food supply; failure of any single system means death.
q7_justification: Only three Ares missions have been sent to Mars with small crews; the first warranted parades while the second merited only a handshake, indicating space travel remains an exceptional and rare event.
q8_justification: Only NASA (the US government space agency) is mentioned as operating on Mars; there is no reference to rival nations or multinational space governance.
q9_justification: The entire chapter establishes a survival-thriller premise: a man stranded alone on Mars with 31 days of supplies, no communications, and multiple life-threatening failure modes.
q10_justification: The mission is run by NASA as a scientific and exploratory program; no military framing, military personnel, or military objectives are mentioned.
q11_justification: The narrative invokes frontier exploration language ('expand the horizons of humanity') and uses a colonial expedition model with supply pre-positioning and advance unmanned missions before human arrival.
q12_justification: The Martian surface is lethal without a pressurized suit; the atmosphere is unbreathable CO2; sandstorms reach 175 kph; and complex technological systems are required just to breathe, drink, and stay warm.
